task_summary: Characterize the extremophile protein-of-unknown-function MJ0927 (DUF34/NIF3 family) from the hyperthermophilic archaeon Methanocaldococcus jannaschii: build a cross-domain phylogenetic tree, trace conserved dinuclear metal-binding (catalytic) residues, reconstruct ancestral sequences, and infer ancestral function.

steps.title: Select protein and fetch representative homologs
steps.description: Commit to MJ0927 (UniProt Q58337) from Methanocaldococcus jannaschii as the focal extremophile DUF34/NIF3 protein. Fetch its sequence plus a diverse, manually curated set of ~40-60 family members spanning Archaea (incl. thermophiles Pyrococcus, Thermococcus, Sulfolobus, Thermus-adjacent), Bacteria (E. coli YbgI, Bacillus, Deinococcus, Aquifex, Thermotoga), and Eukarya (human NIF3L1, mouse, yeast NIF3, plant, Dictyostelium) via the UniProt REST API (family: NIF3/Pfam PF01784). Save sequences as nif3_homologs.fasta and a metadata table nif3_metadata.csv (accession, organism, domain of life, lifestyle/optimal growth temp where known).

steps.title: Set up bioinformatics environment
steps.description: Create a conda env 'phylo' with biopython, mafft, trimal, iqtree (v2), fasttree (fallback), logomaker, matplotlib, seaborn, ete3/dendropy or Bio.Phylo for tree handling. Verify each binary runs.

steps.title: Multiple sequence alignment and trimming
steps.description: Align nif3_homologs.fasta with MAFFT L-INS-i (accuracy mode). Trim poorly aligned columns with trimAl (gappyout/automated) to produce nif3_aligned.fasta and nif3_trimmed.fasta. Render an alignment overview figure (alignment_overview.png) highlighting the conserved NIF3 core and the metal-binding motif columns.

steps.title: Build maximum-likelihood phylogenetic tree
steps.description: Run IQ-TREE on the trimmed alignment with ModelFinder (-m MFP) and 1000 ultrafast bootstrap replicates (-bb 1000) plus SH-aLRT. Produce nif3.treefile. Render a publication-quality tree (nif3_tree.png) colored by domain of life (Archaea/Bacteria/Eukarya) with bootstrap support shown and the extremophile MJ0927 and key reference sequences (YbgI, NIF3L1, yeast NIF3) labeled. Note whether topology recovers the three domains and where thermophiles fall.

steps.title: Map and trace catalytic/metal-binding residues
steps.description: Using the E. coli YbgI dinuclear metal center as the reference anchor (conserved His/Glu/Asp residues coordinating the two metal ions), locate the homologous alignment columns and extract the residue state for every sequence. Produce a per-domain conservation figure: a sequence logo (metal_site_logo.png) of the metal-binding columns split by Archaea/Bacteria/Eukarya, and a heatmap/table (catalytic_residues.csv) of residue identity per sequence at each catalytic position. Report conservation vs. substitution of each catalytic residue in the extremophile MJ0927 and across domains.

steps.title: Ancestral sequence reconstruction
steps.description: Run IQ-TREE ancestral state reconstruction (-asr) on the tree + trimmed alignment to infer marginal ancestral sequences at internal nodes. Extract ancestral states specifically at the catalytic/metal-binding columns for deep nodes: the last common ancestor of each domain and the deepest (near-root) node approximating the ancestral NIF3 state. Save ancestral_states.csv and a figure (ancestral_residues.png) showing the reconstructed metal-site residues mapped onto key internal nodes with posterior probabilities.

steps.title: Infer ancestral function and synthesize
steps.description: Integrate the conservation pattern, tree topology, and ancestral reconstruction to argue for the most parsimonious ancestral function: whether the dinuclear metal-binding site (and hence a metal-chaperone/insertase or metallohydrolase-type activity) was present in the ancestral NIF3 protein, and how/where the GTP-cyclohydrolase annotation fails. Assemble a final markdown report (nif3_report.md) embedding all figures, with a residue-level evolutionary narrative, the ancestral-function hypothesis, caveats, and a references section. Save all artifacts.

feasibility.rationale: Sequences are freely available from UniProt across all three domains of life, and the full pipeline (MAFFT alignment, trimming, IQ-TREE ML tree with model selection + ultrafast bootstrap, ancestral sequence reconstruction) runs comfortably on local CPU for a few dozen sequences. The catalytic/metal-binding residues are documented from the YbgI crystal structure, giving a concrete anchor. The main uncertainty is functional inference, which is inherently hypothesis-level for a DUF, so conclusions will be framed as well-supported hypotheses rather than proof.
